new_terms.context: AMD's next-generation GPU series used as a benchmark against Nvidia's VR NVL72.
AMD's rack-scale system equivalent to Nvidia's NVL series.
A financial metric used to evaluate the profitability of AI infrastructure investments, agnostic to depreciation.
Thermal Design Power, used to calculate operating costs and power density at the rack level.
Standard floating-point operations per second without using sparsity techniques.
Nvidia's performance metric utilizing 4-bit floating point with sparsity for higher marketed throughput.
AMD's GPU architecture for data centers, mentioned regarding the removal of sparsity support.
A period of increasing prices and demand in the DRAM/NAND market.
Effective dense PFLOP can differ based on Model Flops Utilization % (MFU)
real world use of FP4 Sparsity will probably not reach 50 PFLOPS
it will probably deliver better effective FLOPs than FP4 Dense
measured inference token throughput from our InferenceX benchmarks
delivering roughly 6.3x the performance at 100 interactivity for Deepseek R1
While the B300 is rated at 4,500 Dense FP8 TFLOPs
versus 5,000 Dense FP8 TFLOPs for MI355
meaning spec-sheet compute does not directly translate into real-world token generation performance
